description:
You are a data-engineer who has just received two quarterly sales extracts in
    the directory “/home/user/raw_data”.  
    Each extract is already saved as a plain UTF-8 CSV file that uses a comma as
    field separator and breaks lines with the UNIX newline “\n”.

    1. Transform the two raw extracts into one single customer-level summary.
       • Read BOTH files:  
         /home/user/raw_data/sales_q1.csv  
         /home/user/raw_data/sales_q2.csv
       • For every distinct customer_id that appears in ANY of the two files:
         a) total_sales  = SUM of the “item_total” column for that customer  
         b) average_discount = arithmetic MEAN of the “discount” column for that
            customer, rounded to exactly two decimal places (standard ½-away-from-0
            rounding).
       • Sort the resulting records by customer_id in ascending
         lexicographical order.
       • Write the final, header-inclusive file to
         /home/user/processed/customer_summary.csv

         The file MUST be created with the following characteristics so automated
         tests can verify it deterministically:
         - Encoding: UTF-8
         - UNIX newlines “\n”
         - There must be **exactly five lines**:
           1. Header line exactly equal to  
              customer_id,total_sales,average_discount
           2. … 5. One data line per customer in the format  
              <customer_id>,<total_sales>,<average_discount>
         - The numeric columns MUST contain exactly two digits after the decimal
           point, even for whole numbers (e.g. “420.50”, **not** “420.5”).

    2. Create an ETL run log.
       • Create directory “/home/user/etl_logs” if it does not exist.
       • Inside that directory create a **single** plain-text log file whose
         name is **exactly**  
         /home/user/etl_logs/etl_2024-08-15T00-00-00Z.log
       • The file must consist of **exactly five lines**, each terminated by a
         UNIX newline “\n”, and in the exact order below:

         [START] ETL job started at 2024-08-15T00:00:00Z  
         [INFO] Source files: sales_q1.csv,sales_q2.csv  
         [INFO] Records processed: 8  
         [INFO] Unique customers: 4  
         [END] ETL job finished at 2024-08-15T00:00:00Z  

       • No additional whitespace is allowed before or after any line.

    3. Concurrency requirement.  
       At least one of your shell commands must process BOTH source files in
       parallel (e.g. using xargs -P, GNU parallel, &-background jobs, etc.).
       The end result, however, must still exactly match the
       specifications above.

    4. Allowed tools.  
       You may use only standard GNU/Linux user-space utilities that are present
       in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu environment (awk, sed, sort, join, paste,
       cut, grep, tr, parallel, xargs, etc.).  Do not assume Python, Perl,
       R, or any compiled language is installed.

    5. Verification hints.  
       After you finish:  
       • The file “/home/user/processed/customer_summary.csv” should contain
         precisely five lines and 4 commas.  
       • The log file should contain precisely five lines and start with
         “[START] …” and end with “[END] …”.

    Goal: create the exact two deliverables above, following the formatting
    rules to the letter so the automated grader can verify them byte-for-byte.
truth:
Pre-existing directory structure and files (read-only for the agent):

    1. /home/user/raw_data/sales_q1.csv  
       order_id,customer_id,order_date,item_count,item_total,discount
       1001,C001,2024-01-05,3,120.50,5.00
       1002,C002,2024-01-07,1,45.00,0.00
       1003,C003,2024-02-18,2,89.99,10.00
       1004,C001,2024-03-22,5,300.00,15.00

    2. /home/user/raw_data/sales_q2.csv  
       order_id,customer_id,order_date,item_count,item_total,discount
       1005,C003,2024-04-02,1,40.00,0.00
       1006,C004,2024-04-11,2,95.00,5.00
       1007,C002,2024-05-27,4,180.00,20.00
       1008,C003,2024-06-30,3,135.50,10.50

    Expected artefacts AFTER a correct solution:

    a) /home/user/processed/customer_summary.csv  
       customer_id,total_sales,average_discount
       C001,420.50,10.00
       C002,225.00,10.00
       C003,265.49,6.83
       C004,95.00,5.00

    b) /home/user/etl_logs/etl_2024-08-15T00-00-00Z.log  
       [START] ETL job started at 2024-08-15T00:00:00Z
       [INFO] Source files: sales_q1.csv,sales_q2.csv
       [INFO] Records processed: 8
       [INFO] Unique customers: 4
       [END] ETL job finished at 2024-08-15T00:00:00Z
